title: Must you inform your future spouse if you do not want children?
stanceSummary: If infertility is medical fact, concealing it from a prospective spouse is deceit and wrong. If one is fertile but strongly prefers no children, one should not hide that from the fiancé(e); marriage aims commonly include children, and mutual expectations should be clear—while remaining humble that feelings can change over time.
alternateQuestions: Is it haram to hide not wanting kids?
Must I tell fiancé I don't want children?
transcript:
## Summary of Yasir Qadhi's Position

In a [2020 video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=n0MWNIQh08o) (starting at [0:00](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=n0MWNIQh08o&t=0)), Yasir Qadhi addresses this question.

- **Medical infertility**: **obligatory** to disclose; hiding it is **deception**.
- **Elective childlessness**: should **not be concealed** from a spouse-to-be; many people strongly want children.
- Humility: preferences at age 20–25 **may change** later in life.

---

insha allah you know you can work together to solve that issue and allah knows best  foreign
